gold_rationale: MO-4 changes 'S88' to '(deleted)' at SCRNGATE1:SANCREG.cpy:6-6, producing D4_stale_reference_data. MO-4 drops the registry entry standing for the Taliban Sanctions List (para 56(ii)), so one of the two mandated UNSC lists is no longer screened -- a missing mandated list. The code-side identifier is a synthetic registry code, so the comparison is against registry completeness (both mandated sources present) rather than list membership.

regulation_clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

high_risk.note: Inverted CC-10a trap: the English says 'at least once every N years' (floor on FREQUENCY) but this leaf stores the INTERVAL, so the encoded quantity is a ceiling (at_most) and stale snaps UP (refreshing less often = laxer). Discriminator is the encoded quantity, NOT the clause's phrasing - 'at least' produced a floor on CC-10a and a ceiling here; pattern-matching on the words gets exactly one of them right.

low_risk.note: 10y is the loosest interval the lineage ever ran: no laxer value exists, so MO-1 rejects rather than emit the strict side.

regulation_clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

high_risk.note: Inverted CC-10a trap: the English says 'at least once every N years' (floor on FREQUENCY) but this leaf stores the INTERVAL, so the encoded quantity is a ceiling (at_most) and stale snaps UP (refreshing less often = laxer). Discriminator is the encoded quantity, NOT the clause's phrasing - 'at least' produced a floor on CC-10a and a ceiling here; pattern-matching on the words gets exactly one of them right.

low_risk.note: 10y is the loosest interval the lineage ever ran: no laxer value exists, so MO-1 rejects rather than emit the strict side.

regulation_clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

high_risk.note: Inverted CC-10a trap: the English says 'at least once every N years' (floor on FREQUENCY) but this leaf stores the INTERVAL, so the encoded quantity is a ceiling (at_most) and stale snaps UP (refreshing less often = laxer). Discriminator is the encoded quantity, NOT the clause's phrasing - 'at least' produced a floor on CC-10a and a ceiling here; pattern-matching on the words gets exactly one of them right.

low_risk.note: 10y is the loosest interval the lineage ever ran: no laxer value exists, so MO-1 rejects rather than emit the strict side.

regulation_clause.text: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.

current_value.note: Fixed statutory multiplier (2x charges reversed), not a directional threshold: a stale value is simply WRONG, not laxer/stricter on a grid. mo1_mode=exact_wrong with declared wrong-set [1], not [1,3]: the drift-direction principle extends to constants on two grounds - (a) D1 is temporal staleness and penalty provisions ratchet upward (every primary-verified change in this corpus moved toward strictness), so the plausible FORMER value sits below; (b) survival bias - drift that overpays (3x) is noticed and corrected internally, drift that underpays (1x) generates no internal signal and persists. The mechanism is indifferent to threshold-vs-constant.

regulation_clause.text: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.

current_value.note: Fixed statutory multiplier (2x charges reversed), not a directional threshold: a stale value is simply WRONG, not laxer/stricter on a grid. mo1_mode=exact_wrong with declared wrong-set [1], not [1,3]: the drift-direction principle extends to constants on two grounds - (a) D1 is temporal staleness and penalty provisions ratchet upward (every primary-verified change in this corpus moved toward strictness), so the plausible FORMER value sits below; (b) survival bias - drift that overpays (3x) is noticed and corrected internally, drift that underpays (1x) generates no internal signal and persists. The mechanism is indifferent to threshold-vs-constant.
